Positive prompt. Describe overall quality.

This node adds combines the prompts together.
Get filename from original file. This will be used for the output files.

sampling methods:

ccsr = normal, untiled, heavy on VRAM

ccsr_tiled_mixdiff = tiled, each tile decoded separately, has seams

ccsr_tiled_vae_gaussian_weights = seams fixed, can be less accurate and introduce extra noise
CCSR model used for CCSR. Get it on their github page.
Upscale by a factor of 2. Works best if your original image is less than 2k.
Resize the original image and use that as a color reference for the color fix. Tiled diffusion methods tends to create color shifts. We want our colors to be consistent.
Base model for SUPIR upscale. Realvis XL works well. You can try other models.

SUPIR Sampler
Restore EDM sampler gives the best results in my testing. Even though the recommended settings for Realviz XL is 6 steps - it stills leaves a lot of noise. 24 steps gives the best quality.



REALVIS XL recommended settings.
Use Lightning models with DPM++ SDE Karras / DPM++ SDE sampler, 4-6 steps and CFG Scale 1-2

SD Ultimate upscale brings us to 8k

Again we use Realviz XL but you can try other models.
Controlnet model to guide the diffusion.
Upscale model to bring us to 8k. NMKD 8x Faces works really good. You can try other upscale models.

realviz xl - NOTES

Use Turbo models with DPM++ SDE Karras sampler, 4-10 steps and CFG Scale 1-2.5

Use Lightning models with DPM++ SDE Karras / DPM++ SDE sampler, 4-6 steps and CFG Scale 1-2


Recommended Negative Prompt:

(worst quality, low quality, illustration, 3d, 2d, painting, cartoons, sketch), open mouth

or another negative prompt

ᅠ

Recommended Generation Parameters:
Sampling Steps: 15-30+
Sampling Method: DPM++ 2M Karras / or another

ᅠ

Hires Fix Parameters:

Upscaler: 4x-UltraSharp upscaler / or another

Denoising strength: 0.1-0.5

Upscale by: 1.1-1.5

ᅠ

Optional Parameters:

ENSD: 31337

blur strength 4 -5 gives the best results
high sharpness but large halo
needs more experimentation
Play with the denoise value. The higher the denoise, more "detail" it adds.
